text.title: TDD Antipatterns
text.summary: Agent failure modes in TDD: skip-RED, mock-everything, weak assertions, gold-plating, skipped refactor.

antipattern.description: Agent writes implementation and tests in the same turn, producing tests that trivially pass against the new code.
antipattern.reason: This is rationalization, not verification. The test was written after seeing the implementation; it cannot catch bugs in that implementation.

rule.statement: Require test failure output as part of the RED phase response. If the agent does not produce a failing test runner output, reject the turn.

antipattern.description: Writing assert result is not None for a function that must return a specific structured value.
antipattern.reason: Passes against any non-None return. Catches no behavioral regression.

antipattern.description: Writing assert result == True (bare boolean) instead of assert result.status == 'active'.
antipattern.reason: Both pass if the service returns the wrong object with a truthy value.

antipattern.description: Mocking datetime.now, file I/O, and the system under test in unit tests.
antipattern.reason: Tests become tautological: they only verify that mocks are called in the expected way, not that the code behaves correctly.

rule.statement: Mock only clearly external boundaries (HTTP, S3, third-party SDK, time). In-process services must be called without mocking.

antipattern.description: Adding features not required by the current failing test during the GREEN phase.
antipattern.reason: Gold-plating during GREEN adds untested code. Every line not covered by a failing test is a liability, not a feature.

section.title: Skipped Refactor

antipattern.description: Treating green tests + ugly code as done. The agent never revisits the implementation for clarity, duplication removal, or naming improvement.
antipattern.reason: Technical debt accumulates multiplicatively. The refactor step is the only point in the cycle where the agent can improve code without breaking behavior.

section.reference: Single-behavior loop driver that enforces RED-GREEN-REFACTOR sequence
